name: HapWare
description: HapWare develops AlEye, a wearable assistive technology that translates nonverbal communication cues--facial expressions, gestures, and body language--into real-time haptic feedback. Designed for people who are blind, low-vision, autistic, deaf, and deafblind, AlEye empowers users to navigate conversations, interviews, and social settings with greater clarity and confidence. Our mission is to provide accessibility in every interaction.
program: Techstars San Diego powered by San Diego State University

name: Such Much AI
description: Such Much AI is a no-code procurement process AI automation platform. We empower organizations of all sizes to harness the power of AI without needing technical expertise. Our platform enables users to create AI-driven procurement or public tender workflows with a library of prompt templates or our proprietary AI agent based on open-source large language models (LLM).
